title: Induction therapy of pralsetinib followed by conversion surgery for unresectable locally advanced non-small cell lung cancer with the rearranged during transfection (RET) fusion gene mutation: protocol for a single-arm phase II prospective trial (the SALVAGE-P trial).
abstract: Selective rearranged during transfection (RET) inhibitors have been approved as first-line treatment for locally advanced non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC) harboring RET fusion gene mutations. Growing evidence shows that loco-regional therapy including salvage surgery may improve the prognosis of locally advanced NSCLC after induction therapy. Therefore, we initiated the SALVAGE-P trial to assess the safety and feasibility of induction selective RET inhibitors followed by conversion surgery in treating initially unresectable locally advanced NSCLC.
trial_name: Phase II studies of pemetrexed plus carboplatin
